Create a Dockerfile for this Next.js project

```tool
{"toolCalls":[{"toolName":"write_file","input":{"path":"Dockerfile","content":"FROM node:20-alpine AS base

FROM base AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

FROM base AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM base AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/static ./.next/static
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
"}}]}
```

TASK: Fix the edit_file tool bug — str.replace() does not accept encoding kwarg

```tool
{"toolCalls":[{"toolName":"read_file","input":{"path":"forgeagent/tools/registry.py","start_line":74,"end_line":88}}]}
```

Let me read the edit_file tool to find the bug.

[Tool Result: read_file]
forgeagent/tools/registry.py (3 lines)
85	        content = fp.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
86	        fp.write_text(content.replace(old, new, 1, encoding='utf-8'), encoding='utf-8')
87	        return f'Edited {fp} (1 replacement)'

Found it — line 86 passes `encoding` to `str.replace()` which doesn't accept it. The `encoding` should only be on `write_text()`.

```tool
{"toolCalls":[{"toolName":"edit_file","input":{"path":"forgeagent/tools/registry.py","old_text":"fp.write_text(content.replace(old, new, 1, encoding='utf-8'), encoding='utf-8')","new_text":"fp.write_text(content.replace(old, new, 1), encoding='utf-8')"}}]}
```
